target_desc: status and currency are raw strings compared by value; they are domain concepts that deserve enums
an abstract base with one impl and an unused **options hook, added for hypothetical future formats
long getCustomer().getAddress().getCountry().getIsoCode() navigation chain
every UserService method just delegates to repo with no added behavior
InvoiceFormatter.format reaches into Order data (lines, tax_rate, discount_rate, id) far more than its own; the method belongs on Order
change_note: formatter added next to existing Order
code:
# order.py (existing)
class Order:
    def __init__(self, id, lines, tax_rate, discount_rate):
        self.id = id
        self.lines = lines
        self.tax_rate = tax_rate
        self.discount_rate = discount_rate

# invoice.py (new)
class InvoiceFormatter:
    def format(self, order):
        net = sum(l.price * l.qty for l in order.lines)
        tax = net * order.tax_rate
        disc = net * order.discount_rate
        return f"{order.id}: net={net} tax={tax} disc={disc} total={net + tax - disc}"

target_desc: one logical change (add currency) forces small scattered edits across four files; the concept is not localized
change_note: this single commit adds currency support across four files
code:
# model.py
class Order:
    def __init__(self, total, currency):   # + currency
        self.total = total
        self.currency = currency

# serializer.py
def to_json(o):
    return {"total": o.total, "currency": o.currency}   # + currency

# validator.py
def validate(o):
    assert o.currency in ("USD", "EUR")   # + currency

# formatter.py
def display(o):
    return f"{o.total} {o.currency}"   # + currency

ReadOnlyList subclasses list but rejects most inherited mutators; inheritance is the wrong tool, prefer composition
a small, well-named, single-purpose function with no smell; flagging anything here is a false positive
code:
def is_overdue(invoice, today):
    return invoice.due_date < today and not invoice.paid

target_desc: only TWO near-identical copies exist; the rule-of-three says do not abstract yet, so Duplicated Code must NOT fire
the raw string arrives at a system boundary and is parsed into a type immediately (parse-dont-validate); nothing primitive is left dangling; Primitive Obsession must NOT fire
the interface has one impl today but the seam is genuinely needed: a test mocks it and a provider swap is planned; Speculative Generality must NOT fire
exactly ONE switch in ONE place; Repeated Switches requires the same switch recurring across sites, so it must NOT fire
notify_shipped touches order.customer_email once and works mainly with its own collaborator (mailer); a single brief access is not Feature Envy
template, context, output_path co-occur in exactly one rendering call and are not a recurring related group; Data Clumps must NOT fire
code:
def render(template, context, output_path):
    html = template.format(**context)
    with open(output_path, "w") as f:
        f.write(html)

target_desc: dot, a, b, x, y are conventional, idiomatic math names that are clear in context; Mysterious Name must NOT fire
code:
def dot(a, b):
    return sum(x * y for x, y in zip(a, b))

target_desc: AdminUser uses the parent, overrides one method, and adds new behavior; it honours the inheritance, so Refused Bequest must NOT fire
